text: Setting clear goals is essential for any project’s success. A useful approach is to set SMART goals, which are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. This helps you define exactly what needs to be done and how to track progress. It’s also important to break the project into smaller milestones, which are key checkpoints along the way. Each milestone should have clear deliverables, such as a report or a prototype. Finally, make sure all stakeholders—everyone involved or affected by the project—agree on these goals. Stakeholder alignment prevents confusion and ensures everyone is working toward the same end result.
